# pyaltibase

> Pure Python DB-API 2.0 driver for the Altibase relational database, providing direct connectivity without native client libraries.

pyaltibase is a lightweight, pure-Python database driver implementing PEP 249 (DB-API 2.0) for Altibase RDBMS. It handles wire-protocol communication, type conversion, connection management, and parameterized queries. Default connection: `altibase://sys:@localhost:20300/mydb`.

## Core Documentation

- [Installation](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/installation/): pip install, prerequisites, verification
- [Quick Start](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/quickstart/): First connection, basic CRUD, parameterized queries
- [Connection Guide](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/connection/): URL formats, connection parameters, pooling, timeouts
- [API Reference](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/api/): Module, Connection, Cursor classes, DB-API 2.0 globals
- [Type Mapping](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/types/): Altibase-to-Python type conversion, custom type handling

## Reference

- [Error Handling](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/errors/): Exception hierarchy, error codes, troubleshooting
- [Architecture](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/architecture/): Wire protocol, packet format, connection lifecycle

## Optional

- [Testing Guide](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/testing/): Running tests, test configuration, fixtures
- [FAQ](https://yeongseon.github.io/pyaltibase/faq/): Common questions, compatibility, known limitations
